evals.prompt: I need a CONSORT participant flow diagram for my RCT going to a journal: 500 assessed for eligibility, 150 excluded, 350 randomized into a treatment arm (n=175) and control arm (n=175), with lost-to-follow-up and final analyzed counts.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-schematics: generates the CONSORT flowchart with Nano Banana 2 from the detailed quantitative prompt, with Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview reviewing scientific accuracy, clarity, labels, layout, and professional appearance against the journal document-type threshold (8.5/10) and smart iteration (max 2) that stops early once met. Outputs a versioned PNG in figures/ plus a JSON review log.

assertions.value: Builds the diagram via Nano Banana 2 and applies the journal-tier quality threshold (8.5/10) reviewed by Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, not a hand-drawn or text diagram.

evals.prompt: Create a transformer encoder-decoder architecture diagram for my conference paper, showing the encoder stack on the left, decoder stack on the right, multi-head self-attention, masked attention, and the cross-attention connection between them.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-schematics: generates a neural-network architecture schematic with Nano Banana 2 using a layout-specific prompt (encoder left, decoder right, cross-attention link), reviewed by Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview against the conference threshold (8.0/10) with smart iteration up to 2. Output is a versioned PNG and review log.

evals.prompt: Draw the MAPK signaling pathway from the EGFR receptor through RAS, RAF, MEK, ERK to nuclear gene transcription, with each arrow labeled as phosphorylation or activation, for an academic poster.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-schematics: generates a biological pathway diagram with Nano Banana 2 from the cascade description with labeled activation/phosphorylation arrows, reviewed by Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview against the poster threshold (7.0/10) with early stopping when met, output as a versioned PNG plus review log.

evals.prompt: I want a block diagram of an IoT system: sensors feeding a microcontroller, then a WiFi module to a cloud server and a mobile app, with the connections labeled by protocol (I2C, UART, WiFi, HTTPS).
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-schematics: generates a system/block architecture diagram with Nano Banana 2 showing the layered data flow and protocol-labeled connections, reviewed by Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview against the document-type threshold with smart iteration. Output is a versioned PNG and review log.

evals.prompt: Add a flowchart of our experiment decision logic to the README as version-controllable text that renders on GitHub and diffs cleanly in git.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-mermaid. The user explicitly wants a text-based, git-diffable diagram that renders on GitHub, not an AI-rendered PNG — the skill's own description routes text-based Mermaid diagrams to alterlab-mermaid.

evals.prompt: Make me a photorealistic cover illustration of a futuristic neuroscience lab with glowing brain scans for my talk's title slide.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-generate-image. This is a general photo/illustration/artwork request, not a technical or scientific diagram of concepts and relationships — the skill's description routes general photos, illustrations, or artwork to alterlab-generate-image.
